Figure 6: Average relative errors, error estimators, regularization parameters, indices of selected parameters as functions of reduced basis dimension r for Nincre = 1, and nλ = 1.

Figure 7: Supremacy in accuracy of Greedy-Quadratic ROM: FVM solutions, Greedy-Quadratic ROM, and Greedy-Linear ROM approximations (from top to bottom) at t = 0.5, r = 5 are shown for the advection diffusion equation. Columns correspond to parameter vectors µ = (0, 0)T , (1, 0)T , (1, 1)T from left to right.

Figure 5: Supremacy in accuracy of Greedy-Quadratic ROM: FDM solution, Greedy-Quadratic ROM and Greedy-Linear ROM approximations at t = 6 with r = 29 for σ = 0.47915 (from Left to Right) for the linear acoustic wave equation.

Figure 1: The relative linear-/QM-ROM and reconstruction errors as a function of reduced basis dimensions r, for three regularization parameters λ = 1e − 6, 1e4, 1e6 (from Left to Right). For λ = 1e − 6, the QM-ROM is unstable for r ≥ 5, producing infinite relative errors that are not shown in the left panel. For λ = 1e6 in the right panel, the QM-ROM barely outperforms the linear-ROM. Moderate λ = 1e4 provides the best balance between stability and accuracy among these three cases.
Figure 4: Average relative errors, error estimators, regularization parameters, indices of selected parameters as functions of reduced basis dimension r for lsam = 5, Ξλ = 10−3:1:3, and nλ = 2.

Figure 3: Average relative errors, error estimators, regularization parameters, indices of selected parameters as functions of the reduced basis dimension r for different Nincre. Case 1 to Case 3 (Top to Bottom row).
Figure 8: Average relative errors, error estimators, regularization parameters, indices of selected parameters as functions of reduced basis dimension r for nλ = 2.

Figure 9: Supremacy in accuracy of Greedy-Quadratic ROM: High-fidelity solutions, GreedyQuadratic ROM and Greedy-Linear ROM approximations with r = 41 for σ = 12.538 at time t = 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1 (from left to right) for the viscous Burgers’ equation.

Figure 2: Average relative errors, error estimators, regularization parameters, and indices of selected parameters as functions of the reduced basis dimension r for Nincre = 2, r0 = 1, and lsam = 2. Case 1 to Case 3 (Top to Bottom row) and nλ = 2, 3, 4 for Case 1 to Case 3.
